Title: Homologous Drought-Induced 19 Proteins, PtDi19-2 and PtDi19-7, Enhance Drought Tolerance in Transgenic Plants

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Yeast assays showed that full-length PtDi19-2 and the PtDi19-2-N-C fragment have transcriptional activation activity, whereas the N-terminal fragment PtDi19-2-N alone does not, indicating that the C-terminal region is required for transactivation. The non-activating PtDi19-2-N fragment still interacts with PtDi19-7 in yeast, suggesting domain specialization for activation versus protein–protein interaction.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PtDi19-2 is a Di19 family C2H2-type zinc-finger transcription factor from poplar containing conserved zf-Di19 and Di19_C domains and is localized to the nucleus.
2. PtDi19-2 expression in poplar is strongly induced by drought, PEG, NaCl, ABA, and cold, indicating involvement in multiple abiotic stress responses.
3. PtDi19-2 acts as a transcriptional activator with its activation domain located in the C-terminal region and can interact with PtDi19-7 in yeast two-hybrid assays.
4. Overexpression of PtDi19-2 in Arabidopsis increases ABA sensitivity at germination, enhances tolerance to mannitol-induced osmotic stress and soil drought, and improves physiological drought-resistance traits (higher water content, proline, POD activity; lower electrolyte leakage, MDA, and H2O2).
5. PtDi19-2 overexpression promotes ABA-induced stomatal closure and upregulates ABA/drought-responsive genes (AtABF3, AtDREB2A, AtERD1, AtRD29A), supporting a role as a positive regulator in ABA-dependent drought-response pathways.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq dataset E-MTAB5540 used to obtain log2(FPKM+1) expression values of PtDi19 genes in various tissues under abiotic stresses (drought, salt, cold), visualized as heat maps.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of PtDi19-2 in Arabidopsis increases sensitivity to ABA and enhances tolerance to drought/osmotic stress by improving survival, water status, osmotic adjustment, antioxidant activity, and limiting membrane damage and ROS accumulation.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Functions as a Cys2/His2-type Di19 transcription factor with transcriptional activation activity dependent on its C-terminal region; acts as a nuclear transcriptional activator that positively regulates ABA-mediated stomatal closure and drought-responsive gene expression, thereby enhancing cellular osmotic adjustment and antioxidant defenses under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA-dependent drought stress signaling pathway, including promotion of ABA-induced stomatal closure and upregulation of ABA/drought-responsive genes such as AtABF3, AtDREB2A, AtERD1, and AtRD29A in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: Overexpression of PtDi19-2 in Arabidopsis upregulates ABA/drought-responsive genes AtABF3, AtDREB2A, AtERD1, and AtRD29A under drought treatment, consistent with PtDi19-2 acting upstream as a positive regulator in ABA-dependent drought-response pathways. Regulatory relationships: PtDi19-2 -->(activation)--> AtABF3; PtDi19-2 -->(activation)--> AtDREB2A; PtDi19-2 -->(activation)--> AtERD1; PtDi19-2 -->(activation)--> AtRD29A (based on expression induction in overexpression lines).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Di19 (drought-induced 19) protein containing two typical Cys2/His2 zinc-finger domains, with conserved zf-Di19 and Di19_C domains.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nuclear localization in tobacco leaf cells, co-localizing with DAPI-stained nuclei when expressed as a PtDi19-2::GFP fusion under the 35S promoter.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PtDi19-7 (PtDi19-2-N fragment interacts with PtDi19-7 in yeast two-hybrid assays).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Transcriptome and qRT-PCR analyses show PtDi19-2 is strongly induced in poplar, particularly leaves, by prolonged drought; its expression also responds markedly to PEG (20% PEG-6000; ~10-fold induction at 6–12 h), NaCl (200 mM; ~4-fold induction), ABA (100 μM), and cold (4 °C) treatments.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Populus trichocarpa reference genome from Phytozome (accessed 18 January 2022)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (soil drying in pots; prolonged drought in transcriptome data), osmotic stress via 20% PEG-6000 and mannitol (300–400 mM), salt stress (200 mM NaCl), exogenous ABA (0.7–100 μM), and cold stress (4 °C).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought tolerance and ABA-dependent stress signaling in poplar via Di19 transcription factors
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance; osmotic stress tolerance) and ABA sensitivity
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In Arabidopsis, under mannitol (300–400 mM) treatment, WT germination rate decreased by 84.25%, whereas PtDi19-2- and PtDi19-7-overexpressing lines showed smaller decreases of 64.41%, 61.67%, 57.93%, and 61.11%. After 10 days of drought and 3 days rewatering, survival of overexpression lines was 100% compared to 16.6% in WT. Drought-stressed overexpression lines showed higher leaf relative water content, proline content, and POD activity, and lower electrolyte leakage, MDA content, and H2O2 accumulation than WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: PtDi19-2 overexpression promotes ABA-induced stomatal closure in Arabidopsis leaves, which is associated with reduced water loss under drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus deltoides cv. Nanlin 95 seedlings for stress and expression analyses; Arabidopsis thaliana transgenic lines overexpressing PtDi19-2 (OE2-3, OE2-4); Nicotiana tabacum leaves for GFP-based subcellular localization; Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain AH109 for transcriptional activation and yeast two-hybrid assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Populus deltoides cv. Nanlin 95; hybrid poplar 84K (Populus alba × Populus glandulosa) used as experimental materials
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of Di19 genes from the Populus trichocarpa genome; promoter cis-element analysis; RNA-seq analysis of stress-treated tissues; qRT-PCR validation under PEG, NaCl, ABA, and cold; GFP fusion-based subcellular localization in tobacco; yeast GAL4-based transcriptional activation assay of full-length and truncated PtDi19-2; yeast two-hybrid interaction with PtDi19-7; generation and phenotypic and physiological analysis of PtDi19-2-overexpressing Arabidopsis under ABA, mannitol, and soil drought; DAB staining for H2O2; qRT-PCR of ABA/drought marker genes in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis followed by ABA, mannitol, and soil-drought treatments; physiological and biochemical assays (relative water content, electrolyte leakage, proline, MDA, POD activity, H2O2 staining); subcellular localization via 35S::PtDi19-2::GFP in tobacco; yeast GAL4-based transcriptional activation assay; yeast two-hybrid interaction assay with PtDi19-7; qRT-PCR analysis of ABA/drought marker genes in overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a member of an eight-gene Di19 family through genome-wide search of the Populus trichocarpa genome using conserved zf-Di19 and Di19_C domains; candidate gene selection based on RNA-seq and qRT-PCR expression profiles under drought, PEG, salt, cold, and ABA; full-length CDS amplified from poplar cDNA and cloned into expression vectors for functional assays.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PtDi19-2 is a drought- and ABA-inducible Di19 transcription factor from poplar whose overexpression in Arabidopsis markedly enhances drought tolerance and ABA sensitivity by improving water retention, osmotic adjustment, antioxidant capacity, and ABA-mediated stomatal closure. These features make PtDi19-2 a promising candidate gene for molecular breeding of drought-tolerant woody plants and potentially other crops through transgenic approaches that strengthen ABA-dependent drought-response pathways.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PtDi19-7 is especially strongly induced by drought and ABA compared with other PtDi19 genes and its promoter is enriched in MYB and MYC cis-acting elements associated with stress and ABA responses, which may underlie its high stress-inducible expression.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PtDi19-7 is a Di19 family C2H2-type zinc-finger transcription factor from poplar that is among the most strongly induced PtDi19 members by drought, PEG, salt, cold, and ABA, particularly in leaves.
2. PtDi19-7 localizes to both the nucleus and cell membrane and exhibits transcriptional activation activity in yeast.
3. The N-terminal region of PtDi19-2 interacts with PtDi19-7 in yeast two-hybrid assays, indicating that PtDi19-7 can function in a Di19 protein complex.
4. Overexpression of PtDi19-7 in Arabidopsis increases ABA sensitivity at germination, enhances tolerance to mannitol-induced osmotic stress and soil drought, and improves physiological drought-resistance traits (higher water content, proline, POD; lower electrolyte leakage, MDA, and H2O2).
5. Overexpression of PtDi19-7 in hybrid poplar 84K enhances drought tolerance, with higher leaf water and chlorophyll content, better membrane stability, increased proline and POD activity, and longer, heavier roots under drought.
6. PtDi19-7 overexpression promotes ABA-induced stomatal closure in Arabidopsis and ABA-induced lateral bud dormancy in 84K poplar.
7. PtDi19-7-overexpressing Arabidopsis shows upregulation of ABA/drought-responsive genes (AtABF3, AtDREB2A, AtERD1, AtRD29A) under drought, supporting a role as a positive regulator in ABA-dependent drought-response pathways.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling of PtDi19-7 using RNA-seq dataset E-MTAB5540 across different tissues and abiotic stress conditions, visualized as a heat map.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of PtDi19-7 enhances drought tolerance and ABA sensitivity in Arabidopsis and hybrid poplar 84K, leading to improved survival and growth under drought, better water retention and membrane integrity, higher osmoprotectant and antioxidant levels, deeper and heavier roots in poplar, and ABA-induced lateral bud dormancy.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Functions as a Di19 family transcription factor with transcriptional activation activity; localized to nucleus and membrane, suggesting roles in nuclear transcriptional regulation and potential membrane-associated signaling; positively regulates ABA-mediated stomatal closure, lateral bud dormancy, and expression of ABA/drought-responsive genes, thereby enhancing osmotic adjustment and antioxidant defenses and promoting deeper root growth under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA-dependent drought-response pathway controlling stomatal closure, lateral bud dormancy, and expression of ABA/drought-responsive genes such as AtABF3, AtDREB2A, AtERD1, and AtRD29A.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: In Arabidopsis, overexpression of PtDi19-7 leads to upregulation of ABA/drought-responsive genes AtABF3, AtDREB2A, AtERD1, and AtRD29A under drought, consistent with PtDi19-7 acting upstream as a positive regulator of their expression through ABA-dependent signaling. Regulatory relationships: PtDi19-7 -->(activation)--> AtABF3; PtDi19-7 -->(activation)--> AtDREB2A; PtDi19-7 -->(activation)--> AtERD1; PtDi19-7 -->(activation)--> AtRD29A (based on increased transcript levels in overexpression lines).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Di19 (drought-induced 19) protein belonging to the Cys2/His2-type zinc-finger transcription factors, containing conserved zf-Di19 and Di19_C domains.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Localized to the nucleus and cell membrane in tobacco epidermal cells when expressed as a PtDi19-7::GFP fusion under the 35S promoter.
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PtDi19-2-N (N-terminal fragment of PtDi19-2 interacts with PtDi19-7 in yeast two-hybrid assays).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: RNA-seq and qRT-PCR show PtDi19-7 is one of the most strongly induced PtDi19 members in poplar leaves under prolonged drought, and is also highly responsive to salt, PEG (20% PEG-6000), ABA (100 μM), and cold (4 °C) treatments; under cold, its expression reaches more than 30-fold above the control at 12 h.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Populus trichocarpa reference genome from Phytozome (accessed 18 January 2022)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress (soil drying of Arabidopsis and 84K poplar); osmotic stress via mannitol (300–400 mM) and 20% PEG-6000; salt stress (200 mM NaCl); ABA treatments (0.7 μM for Arabidopsis germination, 1 μM for stomatal assays, 5–10 μM for 84K lateral bud dormancy, 100 μM ABA for poplar seedlings); cold stress at 4 °C.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Functional characterization of a poplar Di19 transcription factor in drought tolerance, ABA signaling, and root and bud development
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress tolerance (drought tolerance; osmotic stress tolerance), ABA sensitivity, root development, and lateral bud dormancy
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In Arabidopsis, transgenic lines overexpressing PtDi19-7 exhibited reduced inhibition of germination under 300–400 mM mannitol compared to WT, with germination rate decreases of 64.41%, 61.67%, 57.93%, and 61.11% versus 84.25% in WT. After 10 days of drought and 3 days rewatering, survival of PtDi19-7 (and PtDi19-2) overexpression lines was 100% compared to 16.6% in WT. In 84K poplar, under 8 days of drought, oxPtDi19-7 lines had significantly higher leaf relative water content and chlorophyll content, lower MDA content and electrolyte leakage, higher proline content and POD activity, and greater root length, fresh weight, and dry weight than WT.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: PtDi19-7 overexpression promotes ABA-induced stomatal closure in Arabidopsis leaves and enhances ABA-induced dormancy of lateral buds in 84K poplar.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus deltoides cv. Nanlin 95 seedlings for stress and expression analyses; hybrid poplar 84K (Populus alba × Populus glandulosa) oxPtDi19-7 transgenic lines and wild-type 84K; Arabidopsis thaliana transgenic lines overexpressing PtDi19-7 (OE7-5, OE7-9); Nicotiana tabacum leaves for PtDi19-7::GFP localization; Saccharomyces cerevisiae strain AH109 for transcriptional activation and yeast two-hybrid assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Populus deltoides cv. Nanlin 95; hybrid poplar 84K (Populus alba × Populus glandulosa) used for oxPtDi19-7 transgenic lines
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide Di19 family identification and expression-based candidate selection; qRT-PCR analysis of PtDi19-7 under PEG, NaCl, ABA, and cold; GFP-based subcellular localization in tobacco; yeast GAL4-based transcriptional activation assays; yeast two-hybrid interaction assays with PtDi19-2-N; generation and physiological characterization of PtDi19-7-overexpressing Arabidopsis and 84K poplar under drought and ABA treatments; measurements of relative water content, chlorophyll, electrolyte leakage, MDA, proline, POD activity, root morphology; ABA-induced lateral bud dormancy assays in 84K; stomatal aperture assays under ABA in Arabidopsis; qRT-PCR of ABA/drought marker genes in Arabidopsis overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis and 84K poplar followed by ABA and drought/osmotic stress assays; detailed physiological and biochemical measurements (relative water content, chlorophyll, electrolyte leakage, MDA, proline, POD activity, root metrics); ABA-induced lateral bud dormancy assays in 84K; stomatal aperture assays in Arabidopsis under ABA; subcellular localization via 35S::PtDi19-7::GFP in tobacco; yeast GAL4-based transcriptional activation assays; yeast two-hybrid interaction assays with PtDi19-2-N; qRT-PCR of ABA/drought marker genes in Arabidopsis overexpression lines.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as one of eight PtDi19 genes via genome-wide search of the Populus trichocarpa genome using conserved Di19 domains, followed by promoter cis-element analysis and transcriptome-based expression profiling; PtDi19-7 was prioritized because of its strong induction by drought, PEG, and ABA; full-length CDS was cloned from poplar cDNA into expression vectors for functional studies in Arabidopsis and 84K poplar.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PtDi19-7 is a strongly drought- and ABA-inducible Di19 transcription factor that, when overexpressed, substantially enhances drought tolerance and ABA sensitivity in both Arabidopsis and hybrid poplar 84K by improving water conservation, root system development, antioxidant capacity, and ABA-induced lateral bud dormancy and stomatal closure. These properties make PtDi19-7 a high-value candidate gene for molecular breeding of drought-tolerant poplar cultivars and other woody crops via manipulation of ABA-dependent signaling pathways.
